questionText: Provide details of your exclusion policies related to industries, activities and/or locations exposed or contributing to environmental risks.
questionKo: details of your exclusion policies related to industries, activities 및/또는 locations exposed 또는 contributing to environmental risks.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Exclusion policies are an element of financial sector companies' negative screening processes to reduce portfolio exposure to environmentally vulnerable projects and/or investments, and to implement environmental commitments. Data users are interested in understanding the types of environmental policy exclusions and the effect that these exclusions have had or will have on the organizations' exposure.

columns.text: Country/area the exclusion policy applies to
options: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
columns.popup_help: Please note: "Worldwide" cannot be selected alongside any other option.
columns.textKo: Country/area the exclusion 정책 applies to
optionsKo: South Georgia 및 the South Sandwich Islands
United Kingdom of Great Britain 및 Northern Ireland
columns.popup_helpKo: Please note: "Worldwide" cannot be 선택 alongside 하나 이상 other option.

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes, we have exclusion policies for industries, activities and/or locations exposed or contributing to environmental risks" or "Yes, our framework includes both policies with client/investee requirements and environmental exclusion policies" in response to column "Policy framework for portfolio activities include environmental requirements for clients/investees, and/or exclusion policies" of 4.7.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes, we have exclusion policies for industries, activities 및/또는 locations exposed 또는 contributing to environmental 리스크" 또는 "Yes, our framework includes both policies with 고객/투자대상 requirements 및 environmental exclusion policies" in response to 열 "정책 프레임워크 for 포트폴리오 activities include environmental requirements for 고객/투자대상, 및/또는 exclusion policies" of 4.7.

guidance.text:
Portfolio (column 1) 
The options which appear are driven by the activities you selected in 1.10.
Type of exclusion policy (column 2) 
Add a row for each type of exclusion policy.
If the exclusion is based solely on geography, for example an area important for biodiversity, or a river basin in an area with water stress, select "Other, please specify" here, then specify indicating e.g. "area important for biodiversity", or "water basin", and specify the area in column "Country/area".
Year of exclusion implementation (column 4) 
Select the year you implemented your exclusion policy.
Phaseout pathway (column 5) 
Indicate what type of projects and/or investments the policy applies to, whether it is for new or existing projects.
Selecting "New business/investment for new projects" implies that you would decline to participate in a financing, investment or insurance for a new project that was covered by the exclusion but may still participate in business for a project in which you were already a lender, investor, or insurer.
Selecting "New business/investment for existing projects" implies that you would decline to participate in business for a project covered by the exclusion, even if you were already a lender, investor, or insurer.
Year of complete phaseout (column 6) 
Select the year you completed your phaseout, or the year you will completely have phased out of financing or insuring any activity or region covered by your exclusion policy.
Country/area the exclusion policy applies to (column 7) 
If you selected "Other, please specify" in column 2 "Type of exclusion policy" based on a geographical criterion, for example an area important for biodiversity or a river basin in an area with water stress, select "Other, please specify" here, then specify the area.
Description (column 8) 
Describe the exclusion threshold (industry classification, company exposure, revenue, production, or other), the asset classes/product types/business division the policy applies to, as well as the impact the policy has had on your exposure to the subject of the policy.
The impact on exposure may be expressed as the change in the proportion of your portfolio exposed to the subject of the policy from the baseline (year of implementation).
guidance.textKo: 포트폴리오 (열 1) The options which appear are driven by the activities you 선택 in 1.10. Type of exclusion 정책 (열 2) Add a 행 for each type of exclusion 정책. 만약 the exclusion is based solely on geography, for example an area important for biodiversity, 또는 a river basin in an area with water stress, select "Other, 직접 입력" here, then specify indicating e.g. "area important for biodiversity", 또는 "water basin", 및 specify the area in 열 "Country/area". Year of exclusion 이행 (열 4) Select the year you implemented your exclusion 정책. Phaseout pathway (열 5) Indicate what type of projects 및/또는 investments the 정책 applies to, whether it is for 신규 또는 existing projects. Selecting "신규 business/investment for 신규 projects" implies that you would decline to participate in a financing, investment 또는 insurance for a 신규 project that was covered by the exclusion but may still participate in business for a project in which you were already a lender, investor, 또는 insurer. Selecting "신규 business/investment for existing projects" implies that you would decline to participate in business for a project covered by the exclusion, even 만약 you were already a lender, investor, 또는 insurer. Year of complete phaseout (열 6) Select the year you 완성 your phaseout, 또는 the year you will completely have phased out of financing 또는 insuring 하나 이상 activity 또는 region covered by your exclusion 정책. Country/area the exclusion 정책 applies to (열 7) 만약 you 선택 "Other, 직접 입력" in 열 2 "Type of exclusion 정책" based on a geographical criterion, for example an area important for biodiversity 또는 a river basin in an area with water stress, select "Other, 직접 입력" here, then specify the area. Description (열 8) 설명 the exclusion threshold (industry classification, company exposure, 매출, production, 또는 other), the asset classes/product types/business division the 정책 applies to, as well as the impact the 정책 has had on your exposure to the subject 의 정책. The impact on exposure may be expressed as the change in the proportion of your 포트폴리오 exposed to the subject 의 정책 from the baseline (year of 이행).

guidance.text:
The organization specifies whether and how its policies are linked to absolute and interim timebound, quantifiable and measurable targets that include reference dates/cut-off dates/boundaries/coverage/exclusions.
The organization endorses or aligns its environmental policies and commitments to the most recent and widely accepted scientific interpretation of relevant global environmental treaties, policy goals (e.g., the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, Paris Agreement, Sustainable Development Goals, Planetary Boundaries), and identifies where and how they align.
Financial institutions shall commit, via a publicly available policy, to the immediate cessation of:
All new financial services to projects and companies involved in new coal mines, extensions/expansions of coal mines, or new unabated coal-fired power plants (inclusive of electric utilities and industrial use cases), with the exception of new financing for permanent decommissioning of production activities and capacity; and
All new financial services to new long-lead time upstream oil and gas projects and mid-stream infrastructure dedicated to new long-lead time upstream oil and gas projects.
The applicability of abatement for the purpose of the arrest of financial services to new unabated coal-fired power plants is considered to be at least a 90% reduction of scope 1 and 2 emissions from the associated coal assets of the holding company or project. For carbon capture to be considered part of the 90%, it must be (i) utilized for mitigation products that have century-scale (or greater) lifetimes (i.e., geological carbon capture and storage); and (ii) must not support processes that enable continued fossil fuel extraction and/or development of production capacity.
guidance.textKo: The organization specifies whether 및 how its policies are linked to absolute 및 interim timebound, quantifiable 및 measurable 목표 that include reference dates/cut-off dates/boundaries/coverage/제외사항. The organization endorses 또는 aligns its environmental policies 및 commitments to the most recent 및 widely accepted scientific interpretation of 관련 글로벌 환경 협약, 정책 goals (e.g., the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, Paris Agreement, Sustainable Development Goals, Planetary Boundaries), 및 identifies 여기서 및 how they align. Financial institutions shall commit, via a 공개되어 있음 정책, to the immediate cessation of: All 신규 금융 서비스 to projects 및 companies involved in 신규 coal mines, extensions/expansions of coal mines, 또는 신규 unabated coal-fired power plants (inclusive of electric utilities 및 industrial use cases), with the exception of 신규 financing for permanent decommissioning of production activities 및 capacity; 및 All 신규 금융 서비스 to 신규 long-lead time upstream oil 및 gas projects 및 mid-stream 인프라 dedicated to 신규 long-lead time upstream oil 및 gas projects. The applicability of abatement 에 대한 purpose 의 arrest of 금융 서비스 to 신규 unabated coal-fired power plants is considered to be 최소 a 90% reduction of scope 1 및 2 emissions from the associated coal 자산 의 holding company 또는 project. For carbon capture to be considered part 의 90%, it 해야 합니다 be (i) utilized for mitigation products that have century-scale (or greater) lifetimes (i.e., geological carbon capture 및 storage); 및 (ii) 해야 합니다 not support 프로세스es that enable continued fossil fuel extraction 및/또는 development of production capacity.

guidance.text:
Exclusion policy: a provision that eliminates bank lending and/or insurance underwriting coverage for a certain type of projects and/or companies based on a specific criterion. For investors, it means the exclusion of certain businesses from investment portfolios. Exclusion policies may be framed in the context of a financial sector company's negative screening processes. 
Portfolio: in the context of this questionnaire your portfolio is the entire collection of your core financing activities and insurance policies that you offer. For banking, this is the entire collection of products, securities and loans held on your balance sheet for which you own the receivable stream. For asset managers, this is the entire collection of your products and investments that you hold and/or manage on behalf of your clients. For asset owners, this is the entire collection of products, funds and investments owned and controlled by your company. For investment portfolios, asset managers should consider discretionary investments, those where the company has discretion over investment decision. For insurance underwriting, this is the entire collection of products and insurance policies you provide to your clients. 
Water stress (‘areas with'): a concept that considers physical quantity aspects related to water resources, including water availability. As good practice, a water stressed area should be measured at the catchment level as a minimum. Commonly accepted global indicators to assess areas as water stressed and their thresholds for reporting to CDP include: 
Water availability - risk category greater than ‘High risk': 3.4 ( WWF Water Risk Filter) . WWF recommends that users also take into consideration ‘Medium risk': >2.6. This category is based on a multi-model approach which integrates the best available global water scarcity risk indicators: water depletion, baseline water stress, freshwater quantity target and groundwater availability. In addition, WWF recommends that users take into consideration locations facing 'Drought' risk as these can exacerbate risks related to 'Water Availability'.
Baseline water stress - indicator equal to/greater than ‘High': 40-80% ( WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas) . This refers to ratio of total annual water withdrawals to available renewable water supply. 
Baseline water depletion - indicator equal to/greater than ‘High': 50-75% ( WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas) . This refers to the ratio of total annual water consumption to available renewable water supply.
guidance.textKo: Exclusion 정책: a provision that eliminates bank lending 및/또는 insurance underwriting coverage for a certain type of projects 및/또는 companies based on a specific criterion. For investors, it means the exclusion of certain businesses from investment 포트폴리오s. Exclusion policies may be framed in the context of a financial sector company's negative screening 프로세스es. 포트폴리오: in the context of this 질문서 your 포트폴리오 is the entire collection of your core financing activities 및 insurance policies that you offer. For banking, this is the entire collection of products, securities 및 loans held on your balance sheet for which you own the receivable stream. For 자산운용사, this is the entire collection of your products 및 investments that you hold 및/또는 manage on behalf of your clients. For asset owners, this is the entire collection of products, funds 및 investments owned 및 controlled by your company. For investment 포트폴리오s, 자산운용사 해야 합니다 consider discretionary investments, those 여기서 the company has discretion over investment decision. For insurance underwriting, this is the entire collection of products 및 insurance policies you provide to your clients. Water stress (‘areas with'): a concept that considers physical quantity aspects related to water resources, 포함 water availability. As good practice, a water stressed area 해야 합니다 be measured at the catchment level as a 최소. Commonly accepted global indicators to assess areas as water stressed 및 their thresholds for reporting to CDP include: Water availability - risk category 초과 ‘High risk': 3.4 (WWF Water Risk Filter). WWF recommends that users also take into consideration ‘Medium risk': >2.6. This category is based on a multi-model approach which integrates the best available global water scarcity risk indicators: water depletion, baseline water stress, freshwater quantity 목표 및 groundwater availability. In addition, WWF recommends that users take into consideration locations facing 'Drought' risk as these can exacerbate 리스크 related to 'Water Availability'. Baseline water stress - indicator equal to/초과 ‘High': 40-80% (WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas). This refers to ratio of total annual water withdrawals to available re신규able water supply. Baseline water depletion - indicator equal to/초과 ‘High': 50-75% (WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas). This refers to the ratio of total annual water consumption to available re신규able water supply.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 6/6 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 6/6점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: Table 완성 - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/1점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
The following conditions must be met to be eligible for Management points:
- Full Awareness points awarded
- Any option excluding 'Other, please specify' selected in column 'Phaseout pathway'
ROUTE A)
'All fossil fuels' selected in column 'Type of exclusion policy' AND
Year provided in column 'Year of complete phaseout' is before or the same as 2030 - 2 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
'All coal' selected in column 'Type of exclusion policy' AND
Year provided in column 'Year of complete phaseout' is before or the same as 2030 - 1 point
A maximum of 1/2 points is available for this route.
One row scored.
criteria.criteriaKo: The following conditions 해야 합니다 be met to be 획득 가능 for 관리 점: - 인지에서 최고점 획득 - 하나 이상 option 제외 'Other, 직접 입력' 선택 in 열 'Phaseout pathway' 경로 A) 'All fossil fuels' 선택 in 열 'Type of exclusion 정책' 그리고 Year 입력 in 열 'Year of complete phaseout' is before 또는 the same as 2030 - 2 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다. 또는 경로 B) 'All coal' 선택 in 열 'Type of exclusion 정책' 그리고 Year 입력 in 열 'Year of complete phaseout' is before 또는 the same as 2030 - 1 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/2점입니다. One 행 scored.

simpleHelpKo: Exclusion policies are an element of financial sector companies' negative screening 프로세스es to reduce 포트폴리오 exposure to environmentally vulnerable projects 및/또는 investments, 및 to implement environmental commitments. 데이터 사용자 are interested in understanding the types of environmental 정책 제외사항 및 the effect that these 제외사항 have had 또는 will have on the organizations' exposure.
